lines: The knight raised her sword. The dragon didn't even blink. Something told her this fight would take a while.
The goblin scout froze mid-step. It had heard something in the bushes. It was, unfortunately, correct to be worried.
The chest sat alone in the torchlight. It looked perfectly ordinary. That was exactly the problem.
Rain lashed the castle walls all night. By morning, the moat had become a lake. The guards were not amused.
The wizard opened the ancient book. Dust rose in a thick cloud. Somewhere, a very old joke had just been ruined.
The ogre swung wildly and missed. Twice. The third swing, unfortunately, connected.
Something rattled deep in the dungeon. The torches flickered as one. Nobody wanted to go check.
The map promised treasure just past the ridge. It did not mention the troll. Maps rarely do.
